behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: This is a wiring/contract check between evaluate-stage command logic and artifact persistence (key/slot selection), which can fail at module handoff even if unit tests pass. System is not required because behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess or external round-trip visibility. Agentic/Workflow are rejected because the behavior is about where data is stored, not model judgment quality or multi-agent decision sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a real in-process artifact store and run context; assert a `grounding` artifact is created and `plan` is not written by evaluate.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This validates cross-module orchestration: implement reads one artifact channel and writes another. That handoff is beyond a single-function boundary. System is disqualified (no explicit real infra requirement). Agentic is disqualified by behavior text because correctness here is routing/state usage (`reads grounding`, `writes ... to plan`), not whether model output itself is good.
behaviors.test_description: Seed context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with integrated storage + flow modules, verify grounding is consumed (e.g., surfaced in implement input/state) and output artifact is written to `plan`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: This is a side-effect suppression check across deep-dive decision logic + artifact writer interaction. Integration is needed to catch unintended write behavior at module boundaries. System is rejected because no real external boundary is required. Agentic/Workflow are rejected because behavior text focuses on artifact side effects, not model output correctness or sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already includes diagnosis and a preexisting `plan`; run deep-dive and assert `plan` artifact remains unchanged/no new deep-dive write occurs.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Lineage/run-context propagation is a multi-module state-handoff concern (artifact identity, run scoping, lookup), so Integration is the lowest reliable level. System is not required (no explicit real infra triad trigger). Agentic is considered but rejected because behavior text requires lineage resolution, not evaluation of model decisions/content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate to produce `grounding` in run context R, then execute implement in the same context and assert it resolves the same grounding artifact lineage (id/version/run linkage).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly exercises cross-stage context handoff, which is the integration signal.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent chain and verifies that earlier grounding influences later implementation output. That is sequential, cross-step agentic behavior; unit/integration fakes can miss failures in inter-step model-driven propagation. Agentic alone is insufficient because the requirement is about the chained pipeline, not a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real end-to-end chain (`deep-dive -> evaluate -> implement`) with actual model calls; assert final plan content demonstrably incorporates grounding facts created earlier in the same run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence, matching Workflow.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback-path orchestration plus artifact-store integrity across modules. Integration is needed to verify absence handling and non-target artifact preservation together. System is rejected because no explicit real infra contract/readback is required. Agentic/Workflow are rejected because correctness is fallback/state safety, not model quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement with no `grounding` artifact and with other artifacts preloaded; verify a valid `plan` is produced and all non-plan artifacts remain unchanged.
